title: Use native Windows state roots and owner-only token protection
action: Implement D3 with native Windows roots and a selected explicit owner-only security mechanism. Configuration uses roaming app data; database/runtime state uses local app data; the default allowed root uses an absolute native user profile and fails closed if unavailable. Token creation must establish a protected parent or explicit owner-only DACL before token bytes become visible, never write-then-harden. Every startup validates effective protection before accepting an existing or new token and fails before serving if validation fails. Preserve explicit-config semantics and all Unix/XDG paths/migration rules per D5. Doctor prints only category plus protection status by default; it never prints token content/name, ACL identities, or full sensitive paths. Host tests cover injected selection/failure seams; windows-support-3 owns real NTFS second-user denial.

truths: Windows config, database/runtime state, and default allowed root resolve to absolute native roaming/local/profile locations
Windows does not auto-migrate Unix/XDG state
First-run token creation has no readable pre-hardening window and repeat run preserves the token
Existing tokens are rejected unless effective owner-only protection validates
Protection failure stops before the HTTP listener starts
Linux XDG, HOME, explicit-config, migration, and mode isolation remain unchanged
This cell alone does not establish the second-user NTFS proof or advertise support

artifacts.substantive: platform-native root selection plus race-free create-and-validate token security seams
smallest direct directory/security dependencies required by the selected official mechanism
fail-closed startup and redacted category/protection doctor status

key_links: The same resolved roots feed config creation, database startup, and allowed-root defaults
Token protection validates both existing and newly created files before serving
windows-support-3 invokes the production security function and proves second-user read denial
prohibitions: No token content/name or full sensitive paths in output
No inherited-ACL claim without effective proof
No service, installer, auto-start, packaging, or destructive migration

verify: cargo fmt --all --check && cargo test --quiet config && cargo test --quiet main_migration_seam_obeys_the_cli_mode_matrix && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings

trace.outcome: Native Windows per-user roots and fail-closed owner-only token lifecycle implemented without new packages; output redacted; real Windows proof remains in cell 3.
